headlines.title: Chris Mason: Burnham is fifth PM in four years - now he'll try to buck the trend
headlines.description: Determined to hit the ground running, the former mayor hopes to maintain the leadership style he adopted in Manchester.

headlines.title: A very quick guide to Andy Burnham
headlines.description: Andy Burnham will replace Sir Keir Starmer as PM, after winning the backing of Labour MPs.

headlines.title: Who is Andy Burnham's wife, Marie-France van Heel?
headlines.description: Van Heel, known as Frankie, is a marketing executive who has been in a relationship with Burnham since their university days.

headlines.title: Five headaches Burnham will have to deal with as PM
headlines.description: From defence spending to housing - the next UK leader has a series of challenges to deal with.

headlines.title: Victory for football? Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup
headlines.description: It might seem like a trite cliche, but you won't find many neutrals disagreeing with that verdict on the World Cup final.

headlines.title: Broadcaster and journalist Edward Stourton dies aged 68
headlines.description: Stourton hosted Radio 4's Today programme for a decade and was a founding member of Channel 4 News.

headlines.title: Fire crews battle blaze on Arthur’s Seat in Edinburgh
headlines.description: A huge plume of smoke could be seen from as far away as Fife on Sunday evening.

headlines.title: My fitness tracker knew I was pregnant before I did
headlines.description: Ravika is one of hundreds of women who are noticing early pregnancy signs through the data on their fitness trackers.

headlines.title: Prisons minister Lord Timpson quits government
headlines.description: The businessman said it was an "honour" to serve in the post, but the BBC understands he feels he has achieved all he can in the role.

headlines.title: ‘Everyone's afraid to die’: Struggle to recruit soldiers in Ukraine turns violent
headlines.description: Aggression against recruitment officers has become increasingly threatening, police say.

headlines.title: Are you a parent missing out on £27 a week? Here's how to check
headlines.description: Thousands of new parents fail to claim child benefit until it is too late to receive some of the money, HMRC says.

headlines.title: The small seaside town winning big from TV tourism
headlines.description: Hope Street has drawn tourists from around the world to Donaghadee in Northern Ireland - and its locals are also enjoying being part of the show.

headlines.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
headlines.description: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

headlines.title: How the World Cup has affected your Premier League team
headlines.description: Now Spain have been crowned world champions after victory over Argentina in New Jersey, attention will quickly shift to the new domestic season.

headlines.title: This farmer wanted to quit the cocaine industry - he couldn't
headlines.description: Colombians who grow cocaine's raw material bemoan a lack of support to help them switch crops.

headlines.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at World Cup half-time show
headlines.description: Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, and Shakira entertain the crowd at half-time in the World Cup final, with a special appearance from Ronaldo and Ronaldinho at the New York New Jersey Stadium.

headlines.title: Scaloni leaves news conference in tears talking about his future
headlines.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni leaves his post-match news conference after Sunday's World Cup final defeat by Spain in tears.

headlines.title: Mbappe first to win Golden Boot twice as Rodri named best player
headlines.description: Kylian Mbappe has become the first player to win the World Cup Golden Boot twice after earning the award for top scorer at the 2026 tournament, while Spain's Rodri lands the Golden Ball as the best player.

headlines.title: Trump says US strikes hit Iran in 'honour' of American soldiers killed
headlines.description: Iran responded with attacks in Syria and Jordan, as two ships reportedly exploded in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Tennis players must take sex test to play on women's tour under new WTA policy
headlines.description: The Women's Tennis Association is to ask all players to undergo genetic sex testing in order to compete on the WTA Tour.

headlines.title: Ryanair profits drop as Iran war puts off passengers and lifts fuel costs
headlines.description: The Irish airline said oil prices had increased costs meanwhile Brent crude surpassed $90.

headlines.title: JD Vance says wife Usha and son 'happy and healthy' after birth of fourth child
headlines.description: The couple welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, on Sunday morning.

headlines.title: Norway opens new memorial for victims of 2011 massacre
headlines.description: The artwork in the capital Oslo was unveiled on Sunday ahead of the 15th anniversary of the attack.

headlines.title: Residents told to pay £7,000 each to fix water pipe outside their homes
headlines.description: Welsh Water says it doesn't own the pipe and residents have 11 days to fix it - or face prosecution.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: What No 10 Will Really Be Like For Andy Burnham
headlines.description: Former insiders explain ahead of moving in day.

headlines.title: Will Trump's primetime speech help him challenge the midterm elections?
headlines.description: Join Americast for insights and analysis on what's happening inside Trump's White House.

headlines.title: What Has the BBC Ever Done for Me?
headlines.description: Watch to find out

headlines.title: Lamine Yamal 'completes football' at 19 as Argentina betray Messi
headlines.description: Spain's Lamine Yamal takes the glory of World Cup victory as the great Lionel Messi's evening ends in tears.

headlines.title: Stay or go - at 28, Rashford faces big Man Utd call
headlines.description: The World Cup is over, and Marcus Rashford returns home not knowing if his future at club level lies with Manchester United.

headlines.title: Villa sign Wolves midfielder Gomes in £38m deal
headlines.description: Aston Villa move for Brazil midfielder Joao Gomes after Amadou Onana suffered a serious knee injury at the World Cup.

headlines.title: How the World Cup has affected your Premier League team
headlines.description: Now Spain have been crowned world champions after victory over Argentina in New Jersey, attention will quickly shift to the new domestic season.

headlines.title: Players will need sex test to play on WTA Tour
headlines.description: The Women's Tennis Association is to ask all players to undergo genetic sex testing in order to compete on the WTA Tour.

headlines.title: Victory for football? Spain beat Argentina to win World Cup
headlines.description: It might seem like a trite cliche, but you won't find many neutrals disagreeing with that verdict on the World Cup final.

headlines.title: 'The reaction is terrible' - World Cup final turns ugly at full-time
headlines.description: The frustration of some Argentina players boils over at the end of the 2026 World Cup final, after the 2022 winners are beaten by Spain in extra time.
